title: Fixing Missing Left Child Node Handling in a Tree

statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided as a tree structure represented as a list of edges (parent, child) where parent represents the root of the tree.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a list of nodes that are missing a left child node.
statement.notes: Note that a node is considered to have a left child node if it has at least one child.

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def solve(self, edges: List[List[int]]) -> List[int]:
        # Find all nodes that have at least one child node.
        nodes_with_children = set()
        for edge in edges:
            nodes_with_children.add(edge[0])
        
        # Find all nodes that are missing a left child node.
        nodes = set(range(1, max(max(edge) for edge in edges) + 1))
        result = list(nodes - nodes_with_children)
        return result


root = 1
edges = [[1, 2], [2, 3], [1, 4]]

solution = Solution()
print(solution.solve(edges))
